title: IMCD estimator examples
text: dataset contains interval data car specifications (Duarte Silva Brito (2025)), including min-max values. aggregation microdata done car model, resulting n=27n=27 observations. composed 55 variables: Engine Capacity (EngCap) Top Speed Acceleration Price (lnPrice log transformation) Class (values: Berlina, Luxury, Sportive, Utilitarian) microdata available, following standard assumption literature, adopt continuous uniform distribution latent variables, corresponding symmetric .d. case δ=1/12\delta = 1/12. default setting intData class. IMCD function used compute reweighted IMCD estimates robust squared Interval-Mahalanobis distances observation estimated barycenter. subset size set ⌊0.75×27⌋=20\lfloor 0.75\times 27\rfloor=20, reweighting cutoff “farness” cutoff level 0.90.9. int_outliers function identifies potential outliers based robust distances obtained IMCD estimates, using cutoff criteria. Pairs plot, lower triangular shows scatter plots four variables, outlying observations highlighted red, upper triangular shows interval correlation matrix.  Distance-distance plot classical versus robust squared Interval-Mahalanobis distances. points’ shape represents car model class, horizontal vertical lines correspond 0.9 farness cutoff value 1.5 adjusted boxplot cutoff value, respectively, outliers marked red.
code: data(intCars) cars_microdata <- intCars$microdata cars_int <- intCars$intData cars_IMCD <- IMCD(cars_int, m = floor(0.75*cars_int@NObs), cutoff = "farness", cutoff_lvl = 0.9) cars_outliers <- int_outliers(cars_IMCD$robust_dist, cutoff = "farness", cutoff_lvl = 0.9) cars_outliers$outliers_names #> [1] "Ferrari"         "HondaNSK"        "MercedesSL"      "MercedesClasseS" #> [5] "Porsche" cars_outliers_colors <- rep('gray50', cars_int@NObs) names(cars_outliers_colors) <- rownames(cars_int) cars_outliers_colors[cars_outliers$outliers_names] <- 'red'  SYMB.pairs.panels(cars_int, palette = cars_outliers_colors, type = "rectangles",                      corr = cov2cor(cars_IMCD$cov_IMCD), labels = colnames(cars_int),                     is_outlier = cars_outliers$is_outlier, gap = 0) # Classical distances and outliers cars_class_dist <- IMah_dist(cars_int, z = rep(1,cars_int@NObs)) cars_class_outliers <- int_outliers(cars_class_dist, cutoff = "adjbox", cutoff_lvl = 1.5)  cars_is_outliers <- as.character(cars_outliers$is_outlier) cars_is_outliers[cars_outliers$is_outlier] <- "Outlier" cars_is_outliers[!cars_outliers$is_outlier] <- "Inlier"  plot_dist_dist(cars_class_dist, cars_class_outliers$cutoff_value[[2]], class_cutoff_label = "1.5 Adjbox",                 cars_IMCD$robust_dist, cars_outliers$cutoff_value, rob_cutoff_label = "0.9 Farness",                 color_class = cars_is_outliers, ggplotly = FALSE, shape_class = cars_microdata$class,                  shape_label = "Class", palette = c("gray50","red"),                  label_obs = c(cars_outliers$outliers_names, "Bmwserie7"))

title: IMCD estimator examples
text: dataset contains interval data Spotify tracks’ audio features (Pandya (2022)), including min-max values trimmed intervals, well microdata. aggregation microdata done track genre, resulting n=111n=111 observations. composed 11 audio features: duration danceability energy loudness speechiness acousticness instrumentalness liveness valence tempo popularity Prior aggregation, logarithmic transformations applied loudness tempo, duration_ms milliseconds converted duration minutes, popularity scaled range [0,1][0,1]. latent variables’ parameters estimated microdata, using Kernel Density Estimation (KDE) (package kde1d). , use data aggregated trimmed intervals, taking lower upper bounds 1%1\% 99%99\% quantiles, respectively. IMCD estimates computed using IMCD function subset size ⌊0.75×111⌋=83\lfloor 0.75\times 111\rfloor=83, reweighting cutoff based “farness” cutoff level 0.950.95. int_outliers function applies outlier detection rule using “farness” cutoff 0.950.95 identify strong outliers 0.90.9 mild outliers. Interval correlation matrix plot based IMCD estimates.  Distance-distance plot classical versus robust squared Interval-Mahalanobis distances Spotify dataset. horizontal lines correspond 0.90.9 0.950.95 farness cutoff values, extreme outliers marked red mild outliers orange, vertical line represents 1.51.5 adjusted boxplot cutoff.
code: data(spotify_tracks) spotify_int <- spotify_tracks$intData_trimmed spotify_IMCD <- IMCD(spotify_int, m = round(0.75*nrow(spotify_int)),                        cutoff = "farness", cutoff_lvl = 0.95)  # Strong outliers spotify_outliers <- int_outliers(spotify_IMCD$robust_dist, cutoff = "farness", cutoff_lvl = 0.95) spotify_outliers$outliers_names #> [1] "classical" "comedy"    "grindcore" "sleep"  # Mild outliers spotify_outliers_2 <- int_outliers(spotify_IMCD$robust_dist, cutoff = "farness", cutoff_lvl = 0.9) spotify_outliers_2$outliers_names[!spotify_outliers_2$outliers_names%in%spotify_outliers$outliers_names] #> [1] "ambient"       "chicago-house" "death-metal"   "iranian" # Compute correlation matrix from the robust covariance matrix spotify_corr <- cov2cor(spotify_IMCD$cov_IMCD)  colfunc <- colorRampPalette(c("deepskyblue4", "white", "red4")) corrplot::corrplot(   spotify_corr,   method = "color",   type = "upper",   diag = FALSE,   col = colfunc(200),   tl.col = "black",   tl.srt = 45,   tl.offset = 1,   tl.cex = 1.2,   outline = TRUE,   addCoef.col = "black",   number.cex = 1 ) # Classical distances and outliers spotify_class_dist <- IMah_dist(spotify_int, z = rep(1,spotify_int@NObs)) spotify_class_outliers <- int_outliers(spotify_class_dist, cutoff = "adjbox")  spotify_is_outliers <- as.character(spotify_outliers$is_outlier) spotify_is_outliers[!spotify_outliers_2$is_outlier] <- "Regular" spotify_is_outliers[spotify_outliers_2$is_outlier] <- "Mild Outlier" spotify_is_outliers[spotify_outliers$is_outlier] <- "Extreme Outlier" palette_outliers <- c(   "Regular"         = "gray50",   "Mild Outlier"    = "darkorange",   "Extreme Outlier" = "red" )  plot_dist_dist(spotify_class_dist, spotify_class_outliers$cutoff_value[[2]],                  "1.5 Adjbox", spotify_IMCD$robust_dist,                  c(spotify_outliers_2$cutoff_value, spotify_outliers$cutoff_value),                  c("0.90 Farness", "0.95 Farness"), color_class = spotify_is_outliers,                  color_label = "Outlier Status", palette = palette_outliers, ggplotly = FALSE,                  label_obs = spotify_outliers_2$outliers_names)

title: Class `intData` examples
text: dataset contains interval data credit card expenses (Billard Diday (2006)), including min-max values, centers ranges, centers logranges, microdata. aggregation microdata done person-month, resulting n=36n=36 observations. composed 55 variables: Food Social Travel Gas Clothes creditcard dataset includes following components: microdata: data frame 10001000 rows 77 columns. contains microdata, individual measurements variable observations. min_max: data frame 3636 rows 1010 columns. row corresponds different interval observation, column gives minimum maximum values variable. centers_ranges: data frame 3636 rows 1010 columns. row corresponds centers ranges interval data. centers_logranges: data frame 3636 rows 1010 columns. row corresponds centers logranges interval data. different ways create intData object dataset, depending assumptions latent distribution microdata. example, create intData object using min_max component dataset, assuming continuous uniform distribution latent variables, corresponds symmetric .d. case δ=1/12\delta = 1/12. default setting intData class. Since microdata available, can take closer look distribution latent variables. get_latent_var function can used obtain latent variables observed values, standardizing microdata [−1,1][-1,1] interval. example, use min_max component dataset standardize microdata. aggregation criterion month name, create new variable combines name month use grouping variable standardization process. can visualize distribution latent variables using histograms density plots.  examining distribution latent variables, can assume distributions approximately triangular symmetric. , can create intData object using min_max component dataset, specifying latent distribution “Triang”. intData object contains centers ranges interval data, well parameters latent distribution. centers ranges can accessed using @Centers @Ranges slots, respectively, lower upper bounds can obtained using LowerBounds UpperBounds functions. Alternatively, can create intData object using centers_ranges component dataset, contains centers ranges interval data. latent variables’ parameters, since microdata available, another option estimate parameters directly based microdata setting LatentCase = "General" LatentDist = "KDE" use kernel density estimation latent distribution. case, necessary specify Umicro argument, contains standardized microdata values. majority cases, macrodata constructed microdata. micro2intData function can used create intData object microdata, aggregating microdata according specified criterion. example, aggregate microdata month name, using grouping variable created earlier. also specify latent distribution “General” estimate parameters based microdata. LatentCase argument omitted, assumes latent distribution uniform (.d. symmetric), default setting intData class. also possible specify latent distribution, seen previous examples. Now created intData object, can, instance, compute symbolic covariance correlation matrices interval data using int_cov function. Finally, can visualize interval data using SYMB.pairs.panels function, creates pairs plot interval-valued data. lower triangular shows scatter plots variables, upper triangular shows interval correlation matrix.

title: Analysis of Interval DAta
text: AIDA provides tools analysis interval-valued data, including construction, visualization, robust estimation, outlier detection. package built around intData class designed support methodological research applied workflows involving interval-valued data. AIDA currently includes functionality : Construction interval-valued datasets Interval-valued covariance estimation based Mallows’ distance Interval Minimum Covariance Determinant (IMCD) estimator Robust squared Interval-Mahalanobis distance Outlier detection based robust distances Visualization tools interval data

code: library(AIDA)  # Create an intData object from the iris dataset, using the Species column as  # grouping variable. We also specify the latent distribution as "General" to  # estimate the parameters based on the microdata. data(iris) iris_int <- micro2intData(iris[,1:4], iris$Species, LatentCase = "General")  # Check the parameters of the latent distribution iris_int@LatentParam #> [[1]] #>           [,1]      [,2]      [,3]      [,4] #> [1,] 0.1835903 0.1554921 0.1660354 0.1990067 #> [2,] 0.1554921 0.1381013 0.1461729 0.1683492 #> [3,] 0.1660354 0.1461729 0.1627440 0.1828199 #> [4,] 0.1990067 0.1683492 0.1828199 0.2704900 #>  #> [[2]] #>            [,1]       [,2]       [,3]       [,4] #> [1,] 0.01851852 0.00000000 0.00000000  0.0000000 #> [2,] 0.00000000 0.04199507 0.00000000  0.0000000 #> [3,] 0.00000000 0.00000000 0.02913753  0.0000000 #> [4,] 0.00000000 0.00000000 0.00000000 -0.1802821  # Compute the classical covariance and correlation matrices iris_cov <- int_cov(iris_int) iris_corr <- cov2cor(iris_cov)  # Pairs plot, the lower triangular shows scatter plots of the four variables,  # while the upper triangular shows the interval correlation matrix. SYMB.pairs.panels(iris_int, corr = iris_corr, labels = colnames(iris_int))

title: Interval-valued data Minimum Covariance Determinant (IMCD) estimation — IMCD
code: IMCD(   data,   m = 0,   cutoff = c("farness", "adjbox", "chi-squared", "F-dist", "raw"),   cutoff_lvl = NULL )

title: Interval-valued data Minimum Covariance Determinant (IMCD) estimation — IMCD
text: data intData object containing interval-valued dataset (macrodata). m integer specifying subset size use estimation. Defaults floor(0.75*n). cutoff Indicates cutoff considered reweighting estimates: "chi-squared": traditional 97.5\ "raw": reweighting. "adjbox": Adjusted Boxplots (package robustbase). "F-dist": quantile scaled F distribution (adapted package CerioliOutlierDetection). "farness": "Farness" estimated robust distance (adapted package cellWise). Defaults "farness". cutoff_lvl numeric value specifying level cutoff used. cutoff="chi-squared", cutoff_lvl quantile Chi-squared distribution (default 0.975). cutoff="adjbox", cutoff_lvl coefficient adjusted boxplot (default 1.5). cutoff="F-dist", cutoff_lvl quantile F-distribution (default 0.975). cutoff="farness", cutoff_lvl represents threshold farness, default 0.99. cutoff="raw", cutoff_lvl ignored. value provided, function uses default values associated cutoff method.

title: Interval-valued data Minimum Covariance Determinant (IMCD) estimation — IMCD
text: list containing robustly estimated parameters: mean_IMCD_c Estimated mean centers interval data. mean_IMCD_r Estimated mean ranges interval data. cov_IMCD Estimated covariance (scatter) matrix (int_cov) data. final_z Binary vector indicating inclusion observation reweighted subset. cutoff cutoff method used reweighting. cutoff_value Cutoff value used reweighting. robust_dist Robust distances (IMah_dist) observation. farness_probs Farness probabilities (cutoff set "farness").

title: Interval-valued data Minimum Covariance Determinant (IMCD) estimation — IMCD
text: Loureiro, C. P., Oliveira, M. R., Brito, P., & Oliveira, L. (2026). Minimum Covariance Determinant Estimator Outlier Detection Interval-valued Data. arXiv preprint arXiv:2604.26769. https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26769 Adapted https://github.com/frankp-0/fastMCD. case cutoff=="F-dist" adapted package CerioliOutlierDetection (https://cran.r-project.org/package=CerioliOutlierDetection).

title: Interval-Mahalanobis Distance — IMah_dist
text: vector squared Interval-Mahalanobis distance observation.

title: Interval-Mahalanobis Distance — IMah_dist
text: squared Interval-Mahalanobis distance defined according LatentCase: "U_id_symmetric": latent variables identically distributed symmetric: $$d_{IMah}(\boldsymbol{x})^2=(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)+\delta(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R),$$ \(\delta=\mathbb{E}(U^2)/4\) parameter latent variables. "U_id": latent variables identically distributed: $$d_{IMah}(\boldsymbol{x})^2=(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)+\delta(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)\\              +\dfrac{\mathbb{E}(U)}{2}(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)^\top\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)+\dfrac{\mathbb{E}(U)}{2}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C),$$ \(\delta=\mathbb{E}(U^2)/4\) \(\mathbb{E}(U)\) parameter latent variables. "General": latent variables nice properties: $$d_{IMah}(\boldsymbol{x})^2=(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)+\dfrac{1}{4}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)^{\top}\left(\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}\bullet\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}\right)(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)\\              +\dfrac{1}{2}(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}\boldsymbol{\Psi}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)+\dfrac{1}{2}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Psi}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C),$$ : \(\boldsymbol{\Psi}=\text{diag}(\mathbb{E}(U_1),\dots,\mathbb{E}(U_p))\), \([\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}]_{ij}=\mathcal{E}(U_i,U_j)\), \(\neq j\), \(\mathcal{E}(U_i,U_j)=\int_0^1 F_{U_i}^{-1}(t) F_{U_j}^{-1}(t) \, dt\), \([\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}]_{ii}=\mathbb{E}(U_i^2)\), \(,j=1,\dots,p\), \(\bullet\) denotes Schur (entrywise) product matrices.

title: Interval-Mahalanobis distance for all pairs — IMah_dist_pairs
text: squared Interval-Mahalanobis distance defined according LatentCase: "U_id_symmetric": latent variables identically distributed symmetric: $$d_{IMah}(\boldsymbol{x}_i,\boldsymbol{x}_j)^2=(\boldsymbol{c}_i-\boldsymbol{c}_j)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{c}_i-\boldsymbol{c}_j)+\delta(\boldsymbol{r}_i-\boldsymbol{r}_j)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{r}_i-\boldsymbol{r}_j),$$ \(\delta=\mathbb{E}(U^2)/4\) parameter latent variables. "U_id": latent variables identically distributed: $$d_{IMah}(\boldsymbol{x}_i,\boldsymbol{x}_j)^2=(\boldsymbol{c}_i-\boldsymbol{c}_j)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{c}_i-\boldsymbol{c}_j)+\delta(\boldsymbol{r}_i-\boldsymbol{r}_j)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{r}_i-\boldsymbol{r}_j)\\              +\dfrac{\mathbb{E}(U)}{2}(\boldsymbol{c}_i-\boldsymbol{c}_j)^\top\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{r}_i-\boldsymbol{r}_j)+\dfrac{\mathbb{E}(U)}{2}(\boldsymbol{r}_i-\boldsymbol{r}_j)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{c}_i-\boldsymbol{c}_j),$$ \(\delta=\mathbb{E}(U^2)/4\) \(\mathbb{E}(U)\) parameter latent variables. "General": latent variables nice properties: $$d_{IMah}(\boldsymbol{x}_i,\boldsymbol{x}_j)^2=(\boldsymbol{c}_i-\boldsymbol{c}_j)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{c}_i-\boldsymbol{c}_j)+\dfrac{1}{4}(\boldsymbol{r}_i-\boldsymbol{r}_j)^{\top}\left(\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}\bullet\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}\right)(\boldsymbol{r}_i-\boldsymbol{r}_j)\\              +\dfrac{1}{2}(\boldsymbol{c}_i-\boldsymbol{c}_j)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}\boldsymbol{\Psi}(\boldsymbol{r}_i-\boldsymbol{r}_j)+\dfrac{1}{2}(\boldsymbol{r}_i-\boldsymbol{r}_j)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Psi}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{B}^{-1}(\boldsymbol{c}_i-\boldsymbol{c}_j),$$ : \(\boldsymbol{\Psi}=\text{diag}(\mathbb{E}(U_1),\dots,\mathbb{E}(U_p))\), \([\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}]_{ij}=\mathcal{E}(U_i,U_j)\), \(\neq j\), \(\mathcal{E}(U_i,U_j)=\int_0^1 F_{U_i}^{-1}(t) F_{U_j}^{-1}(t) \, dt\), \([\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}]_{ii}=\mathbb{E}(U_i^2)\), \(,j=1,\dots,p\), \(\bullet\) denotes Schur (entrywise) product matrices.

title: Interval-Mahalanobis distance for all pairs — IMah_dist_pairs

title: Mallows Distance — Mallows_dist
text: squared Mallows distance defined according LatentCase: "U_id_symmetric": latent variables identically distributed symmetric: $$d_{M}(\boldsymbol{x})^2=(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)^{\top}(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)+\delta(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)^{\top}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R),$$ \(\delta=\mathbb{E}(U^2)/4\) parameter latent variables. "U_id": latent variables identically distributed: $$d_{M}(\boldsymbol{x})^2=(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)^{\top}(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)+\delta(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)^{\top}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)              +\mathbb{E}(U)(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)^\top(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R),$$ \(\delta=\mathbb{E}(U^2)/4\) \(\mathbb{E}(U)\) parameter latent variables. "General": latent variables nice properties: $$d_{M}(\boldsymbol{x})^2=(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)^{\top}(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)+(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Delta}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R)              +(\boldsymbol{c}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_C)^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Psi}(\boldsymbol{r}-\boldsymbol{\mu}_R),$$ : \(\boldsymbol{\Psi}=\text{diag}(\mathbb{E}(U_1),\dots,\mathbb{E}(U_p))\), \(\boldsymbol{\Delta}=\text{diag}(\mathbb{E}(U^2_1),\dots,\mathbb{E}(U^2_p))/4\).

text: latent variables, \(U_{ij}\), defined according following model: Let \(X_j=(C_j,R_j)^\top=\left[C_j-\dfrac{R_j}{2}, C_j+\dfrac{R_j}{2}\right]\) represent macrodata $$V_{ij}=C_j+U_{ij}\dfrac{R_j}{2},\quad j=1,\dots,p,\ =1,\dots,m_j$$ microdata \(U_{ij}\) random variables support \([-1,1]\), uncorrelated \((C_j,R_j)\).

title: Interval-valued Covariance — int_cov
text: function calculates interval-valued covariance matrix, \(\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_B\), based covariance matrices centers, \(\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{CC}\), ranges, \(\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{RR}\), covariance matrix centers ranges, \(\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{CR}=\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{RC}^\top\). covariance matrix defined according LatentCase: "U_id_symmetric": latent variables identically distributed symmetric: $$\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_B=\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{CC}+\delta\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{RR},$$ \(\delta=\mathbb{E}(U^2)/4\) parameter latent variables. "U_id": latent variables identically distributed: $$\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_B=\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{CC}+\delta\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{RR}+\dfrac{\mathbb{E}(U)}{2}\left(\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{CR}+\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{RC}\right),$$ \(\delta=\mathbb{E}(U^2)/4\) \(\mathbb{E}(U)\) parameters latent variables. "General": latent variables nice properties: $$\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_B=\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{CC}+\dfrac{1}{4}\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}\bullet\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{RR}+\dfrac{1}{2}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{CR}\boldsymbol{\Psi}+\dfrac{1}{2}\boldsymbol{\Psi}\boldsymbol{\Sigma}_{RC}$$ : \(\boldsymbol{\Psi}=\text{diag}(\mathbb{E}(U_1),\dots,\mathbb{E}(U_p))\), \([\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}]_{ij}=\mathcal{E}(U_i,U_j)\), \(\neq j\), \(\mathcal{E}(U_i,U_j)=\int_0^1 F_{U_i}^{-1}(t) F_{U_j}^{-1}(t) \, dt\), \([\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}]_{ii}=\mathbb{E}(U_i^2)\), \(,j=1,\dots,p\), \(\bullet\) denotes Schur (entrywise) product matrices.

title: Sample Interval-valued Covariance — int_cov_z
text: Let \(\boldsymbol{z}\\{0,1\}^n\) vector indicating \(m\) observations “active”. function calculates sample interval-valued covariance matrix function \(\boldsymbol{z}\): \(\boldsymbol{S}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\). Let \(\boldsymbol{C}\), \(\boldsymbol{R}\) matrices centers ranges, respectively. Additionally, set: $$\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})=\dfrac{1}{m}\boldsymbol{C}^{\top}\boldsymbol{z}, \qquad \overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})=\dfrac{1}{m}\boldsymbol{R}^{\top}\boldsymbol{z}.$$ sample interval-valued covariance matrix obtained according LatentCase: "U_id_symmetric": latent variables identically distributed symmetric: $$\boldsymbol{S}_B(\boldsymbol{z})=\left(\dfrac{1}{m}\sum\limits_{h=1}^{n}z_{h}\boldsymbol{c}_{h}\boldsymbol{c}_{h}^{\top}\right)-\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})^\top+\left(\dfrac{\delta}{m}\sum\limits_{h=1}^{n}z_{h}\boldsymbol{r}_{h}\boldsymbol{r}_{h}^{\top}\right)-\delta\overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})^\top,$$ \(\delta=\mathbb{E}(U^2)/4\) parameter latent variables. "U_id": latent variables identically distributed: $$\boldsymbol{S}_B(\boldsymbol{z})=\left(\dfrac{1}{m}\sum\limits_{h=1}^{n}z_{h}\boldsymbol{c}_{h}\boldsymbol{c}_{h}^{\top}\right)-\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})^\top+\left(\dfrac{\delta}{m}\sum\limits_{h=1}^{n}z_{h}\boldsymbol{r}_{h}\boldsymbol{r}_{h}^{\top}\right)-\delta\overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})^\top\\              +\left(\dfrac{\mathbb{E}(U)}{2m}\sum\limits_{h=1}^{n}z_{h}\boldsymbol{c}_{h}\boldsymbol{r}_{h}^{\top}\right)-\dfrac{\mathbb{E}(U)}{2}\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})^\top+\left(\dfrac{\mathbb{E}(U)}{2m}\sum\limits_{h=1}^{n}z_{h}\boldsymbol{r}_{h}\boldsymbol{c}_{h}^{\top}\right)-\dfrac{\mathbb{E}(U)}{2}\overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})^\top,$$ \(\delta=\mathbb{E}(U^2)/4\) \(\mathbb{E}(U)\) parameters latent variables. "General": latent variables nice properties: $$\boldsymbol{S}_B(\boldsymbol{z})=\left(\dfrac{1}{m}\sum\limits_{h=1}^{n}z_{h}\boldsymbol{c}_{h}\boldsymbol{c}_{h}^{\top}\right)-\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})^\top+\left(\dfrac{1}{4m}\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}\bullet\sum\limits_{h=1}^{n}z_{h}\boldsymbol{r}_{h}\boldsymbol{r}_{h}^{\top}\right)-\dfrac{1}{4}\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}\bullet\left[\overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})^\top\right]\\              +\left(\dfrac{1}{2m}\sum\limits_{h=1}^{n}z_{h}\boldsymbol{c}_{h}\boldsymbol{r}_{h}^{\top}\right)\boldsymbol{\Psi}-\dfrac{1}{2}\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})^\top\boldsymbol{\Psi}+\boldsymbol{\Psi}\left(\dfrac{1}{2m}\sum\limits_{h=1}^{n}z_{h}\boldsymbol{r}_{h}\boldsymbol{c}_{h}^{\top}\right)-\dfrac{1}{2}\boldsymbol{\Psi}\overline{\boldsymbol{r}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})\overline{\boldsymbol{c}}_B(\boldsymbol{z})^\top,$$ : \(\boldsymbol{\Psi}=\text{diag}(\mathbb{E}(U_1),\dots,\mathbb{E}(U_p))\), \([\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}]_{ij}=\mathcal{E}(U_i,U_j)\), \(\neq j\), \(\mathcal{E}(U_i,U_j)=\int_0^1 F_{U_i}^{-1}(t) F_{U_j}^{-1}(t) \, dt\), \([\boldsymbol{\mathfrak{E}}_{UU}]_{ii}=\mathbb{E}(U_i^2)\), \(,j=1,\dots,p\), \(\bullet\) denotes Schur (entrywise) product matrices.

code: int_outliers(   robust_dist,   cutoff = c("farness", "adjbox", "chi-squared", "F-dist"),   cutoff_lvl = NULL,   p = NULL,   z = NULL )

title: Outlier Detection for Interval-Valued Data Based on Robust Distances — int_outliers
text: robust_dist numeric vector containing robust distances observation. cutoff character string specifying method setting outlier cutoff threshold. Options include: "chi-squared": Outliers identified based specified Chi-Squared quantile. "adjbox": Uses adjusted boxplot statistics (robustbase) classify outliers. "F-dist": Applies cutoff derived F Beta distributions robust outlier detection. "farness": Identifies outliers based "farness" threshold, determined robust distance distribution. Default "farness". cutoff_lvl numeric value specifying level cutoff used. cutoff="chi-squared", cutoff_lvl quantile Chi-squared distribution (default 0.975). cutoff="adjbox", cutoff_lvl coefficient adjusted boxplot (default 1.5). cutoff="F-dist", cutoff_lvl significance level identifying outliers (default 0.95). cutoff="farness", cutoff_lvl represents threshold farness, default 0.99. value provided, function uses default values associated cutoff method. p number variables data. Required "chi-squared" "F-dist" cutoff methods. z binary vector indicating subset observations used initial robust estimation. Required "F-dist" cutoff method.

title: Outlier Detection for Interval-Valued Data Based on Robust Distances — int_outliers
text: list following components: outliers_names Character vector names observations classified outliers. is_outlier Logical vector indicating whether observation outlier (TRUE) (FALSE). cutoff cutoff method used detecting outliers. cutoff_value Cutoff value used detecting outliers. farness_probs Numeric vector farness probabilities observation (cutoff set "farness").

title: Outlier Detection for Interval-Valued Data Based on Robust Distances — int_outliers
text: function classifies observations outliers based robust distances user-defined cutoff methods. supports various approaches, including Chi-Squared quantiles, adjusted boxplots, F distribution quantiles, farness probabilities.

title: Outlier Detection for Interval-Valued Data Based on Robust Distances — int_outliers
text: Loureiro, C. P., Oliveira, M. R., Brito, P., & Oliveira, L. (2026). Minimum Covariance Determinant Estimator Outlier Detection Interval-valued Data. arXiv preprint arXiv:2604.26769. https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26769 Case cutoff=="F-dist" adapted package CerioliOutlierDetection (https://cran.r-project.org/package=CerioliOutlierDetection).

title: Outlier Detection for Interval-Valued Data Based on Robust Distances — int_outliers
code: # Example of detecting outliers using robust distances set.seed(42) robust_dist <- abs(rnorm(100)) result <- int_outliers(robust_dist, cutoff="chi-squared", p=5)  # Example using creditcard dataset data(creditcard) credit_card_int <- creditcard$intData  credit_card_IMCD <- IMCD(credit_card_int, floor(0.75*credit_card_int@NObs), "farness", 0.9) credit_card_outliers <- int_outliers(credit_card_IMCD$robust_dist, "farness", 0.9)
